name: Obejo — circular GR48 — Sendero de Sierra Morena
region_intro: Hiking around Obejo offers exploration within the Sierra Morena mountain range, characterized by low hills and peaks. The region features typical Mediterranean vegetation, extensive olive groves, and numerous rivers and streams. This diverse landscape provides varied terrain for hikers seeking tranquil experiences away from urban areas. Best hiking trails around Obejo The most popular hiking route is Obejo — circular GR48 — Sendero de Sierra Morena, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete, offering a circular path through Mediterranean vegetation. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guadanuño Reservoir loop from Base de Cerro Muriano, a moderate 10.0 miles (16.0 km) path. This route features views of the Guadanuño Reservoir and surrounding low hills. Local hikers also love the Cerro Muriano Copper Mines – Piedra Horadada loop from Cerro Muriano, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail leading through historical mining areas and natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Obejo is defined by low hills, Mediterranean vegetation, and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Obejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Obejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Obejo?
A: There are 12 hiking trails around Obejo, offering a variety of experiences from easy walks to more challenging routes through the Sierra Morena landscape.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect when hiking in Obejo?
A: Obejo is nestled in the Sierra Morena mountain range, characterized by low hills, extensive olive groves, and typical Mediterranean vegetation. You'll also encounter numerous rivers and streams, contributing to a tranquil and pristine natural environment.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Obejo?
A: Yes, Obejo offers 3 easy hiking trails. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249387826/obejo-circular-gr48-sendero-de-sierra-morena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obejo — circular GR48 — Sendero de Sierra Morena&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.7-mile route that takes about 1 hour 45 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find more challenging hikes around Obejo?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, there are 3 difficult routes available. The region's varied terrain, with its low hills and peaks, provides opportunities for more strenuous climbs and longer distances.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Obejo?
A: Yes, many trails in Obejo are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249387826/obejo-circular-gr48-sendero-de-sierra-morena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obejo — circular GR48 — Sendero de Sierra Morena&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular path, allowing you to start and end in the same location without retracing your steps.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Obejo?
A: The trails in Obejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 500 hikers have explored the area, often praising its peaceful atmosphere and diverse natural beauty.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while hiking in Obejo?
A: Yes, while hiking, you can discover remnants of Obejo's rich history. The landscape integrates ruins of ancient castles like Castillo de Ubal and Castillo de Peñaflor. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10319954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Muriano Copper Mines – Piedra Horadada loop from Cerro Muriano&lt;/a&gt; takes you through historical mining areas and past the natural monument of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4644760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedra Horadada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that feature reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, the area is known for its water features. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31849563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadanuño Reservoir loop from Base de Cerro Muriano&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadanuño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby reservoirs include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4572001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navallana Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/929932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalmellato Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Obejo a good destination for birdwatching while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The rich natural environment of Obejo, with its diverse vegetation and water sources, makes it an excellent spot for birdwatching. Many hikers enjoy observing the local wildlife as they traverse the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Obejo?
A: Obejo is ideal for 'slow tourism,' encouraging a relaxed pace. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the natural beauty without extreme heat or cold. Summers can be very warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended then.

Q: Are there any natural springs or unique geological features to discover?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural springs. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malos Pasos Spring&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcubilla Spring&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable natural monuments in the area, often providing refreshing stops along the trails.

Q: Are there any nearby parks or protected areas for hiking?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5015538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Villares Periurban Park&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural area near Obejo, offering additional opportunities for hiking and enjoying the forest environment.
tips: Although it is only 47 kilometers from the capital, this Cordovan town feels much further away. Nestled in the Sierra Morena, its orography is partly responsible for its isolation, even the town itself being divided into three nuclei: the historic one, another in Cerro Muriano and another around the station. The historic core, where we are, is very far from the other two.

Of the parish of San Antonio Abad, the tower stands out above all, which is reminiscent of similar constructions from the time of Al-Andalus. From the square there are very nice views of the countryside and the ruins of the castle of Ubal.

Although the castle of Ubal is currently in ruins and only some remains of the towers remain, it is known that it is probably a construction from the Muslim period. It can be seen more closely from the church of San Antonio Abad de Obejo.
